description: A convenient proxy metric is optimized while user-facing quality stagnates or drops, hiding a regression behind a rising number.
body: Failure mode: the team optimizes token overlap or exact-match because it is easy to compute, but the real goal is semantic correctness, so the system learns to maximize the proxy while answers get worse. Detection signals: the metric is a surface proxy unrelated to the goal; metric and human judgment diverge; a single blended number hides per-segment failures. Do not pick a metric for convenience. Safer fallback: state the user-facing goal, choose metrics that correlate with it, pair automatic metrics with human spot-checks, report per-segment, and trust human judgment when it disagrees with the metric.
